arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لَهُ وَارْحَمْهُ وَعَافِهِ وَاعْفُ عَنْهُ
transliteration: Allahummaghfir lahu warhamhu wa'afihi wa'fu anh
translation: O Allah, forgive him/her, have mercy on him/her, grant him/her well-being, and pardon him/her.

insights.meaning: A concise yet powerful supplication for the deceased covering four dimensions: forgiveness, mercy, well-being, and pardon.
insights.benefits: Sending this dua is one of the greatest gifts a living person can give to one who has passed.
insights.when: After any prayer, in Tahajjud, at the grave, or whenever the deceased comes to mind.
insights.explanation: The Prophet (SAW) taught this dua for the deceased. The word 'afw (pardon) goes beyond forgiveness — it means to completely remove and erase sins.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِحَيِّنَا وَمَيِّتِنَا وَشَاهِدِنَا وَغَائِبِنَا وَصَغِيرِنَا وَكَبِيرِنَا وَذَكَرِنَا وَأُنْثَانَا
transliteration: Allahummaghfir lihayyina wa mayyitina wa shahidina wa gha'ibina wa saghirina wa kabirina wa dhakarina wa unthana
translation: O Allah, forgive our living and our dead, those present and those absent, our young and our old, our males and our females.

insights.meaning: A comprehensive supplication encompassing every category of Muslim — the living and the dead, young and old, present and absent.
insights.benefits: This single dua covers your entire community across time and space.
insights.when: During Janazah prayer and whenever making dua for the broader Muslim community.
insights.explanation: This dua was taught by the Prophet (SAW) for Janazah prayer. Its beauty lies in leaving no Muslim behind in one's supplication.
